stimulus: Most discarded fishing nets today wind up in the ocean or are, at best, sent to landfills. To address this problem, materials engineer Hana Mori and colleagues designed a process that breaks down nylon fishing nets and reshapes them into pellets. The pellets can then be used to make thermoplastic (a polymer that softens when heated and can be molded). With this promising new method, abandoned fishing nets could be turned into a range of useful plastic products.
stem: Which choice best states the function of the underlined portion of the text?

answerOptions.content: It identifies a result that confused the team.
It clarifies the meaning of a scientific term.
It explains the significance of a scientific discovery.

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. The underlined portion is set off by parentheses and provides a definition of "thermoplastic"—a polymer that softens when heated. Its function is to clarify the meaning of the scientific term.

Choice A is incorrect. The text addresses an environmental concern overall, but the underlined portion just defines a term. Choice B is incorrect. No confusion is mentioned. Choice D is incorrect. The portion defines a term, not the significance of a discovery.
